title: Keri Russell Wins Best Actress in Drama at 2026 Actor Awards
text: At age 49, Keri Russell claimed the Best Actress in Drama Series award at the 2026 Actor Awards held March 1 at Los Angeles' Shrine Auditorium. She won for her performance as Kate Wyler in Netflix's "The Diplomat," defeating competitors including Britt Lower and Parker Posey. Russell "walked the stage in a sleek black gown and thanked her castmates and the voters, delivering a heartfelt moment that resonated with viewers." The award holds special significance as it's voted on by acting peers rather than industry critics. Russell's win represents recognition for decades of consistent work. She rose to prominence playing Felicity Porter in "Felicity" (earning a Golden Globe at 23), then received three Emmy nominations for her role as Elizabeth Jennings in "The Americans." Her film credits include "Waitress" and "Dawn of the Planet of the Apes." Russell attended with partner Matthew Rhys, her co-star from "The Americans." Both wore coordinated black outfits and became one of the evening's most talked-about pairs.
